content.full_text:
ANNE F. PATTON  
1447 Santa Fe Drive  
Encinitas, CA 92024  
(619) 435-5395

October 30, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 "J" Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Subject: Request for Assistance

Dear Commission:

I am City Council-appointed member of a Design Review Board in the City of Encinitas and as such am considered a "public official." The board reviews development proposals and makes land use decisions.

I work as an independent consultant and am considering representing a firm who does business with the City. I do not own the firm, which is located in another city, nor do I have a financial interest in it. Representation would not involve land use decisions within the City.

Tasks will entail meeting with staff and appearing before the City Council to provide information about the firm and otherwise pursue their interests in a competitive franchise bid situation. I will derive income from this endeavor as consultant to the firm.

Under the circumstances, do I have a conflict of interest? Please advise as soon as possible as the firm is awaiting your decision.

Yours truly,

Anne F. Patton

cc: Roger Krauel, City Attorney
